title: Developing a real pipeline locally: the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm
text: freshly built mock database contains synthetic participants broad spread generic events, won’t contain study’s specific concepts. two exported primitives let put exactly data code needs database: mock_add_concepts() — add rows concept table (concept-name searches concept_id joins find ). mock_add_occurrences() — add clinical-event rows domain table (condition_occurrence, measurement, …), automatically generating surrogate keys matching _ext source-tracking rows. mock_add_occurrences() recycles concept_id date, can pass vector per participant build temporally structured data — pregnancy example . source = "ehr" tags _ext row EHR-based functions (allofus::aou_observation_period()) see event. coarser, prevalence-based seeding also mock_seed_concept_set() mock_seed_survey(), built primitives.
code: library(mockallofus) suppressMessages(library(dplyr))  # build a small database in a temp file for this example con <- mock_aou_connect(path = tempfile(fileext = ".duckdb"), quiet = TRUE)  # define a custom concept, then give it to 20 participants on a specific date mock_add_concepts(con, 99999001, "My custom condition", domain_id = "Condition") people <- mock_person_ids(con) mock_add_occurrences(   con,   domain     = "condition",   person_id  = people[1:20],   concept_id = 99999001,   date       = as.Date("2021-06-01"),   source     = "ehr" )  # the seeded data is now queryable, exactly as real data would be tbl(con, "condition_occurrence") |>   filter(condition_concept_id == 99999001) |>   summarise(n = n()) |>   collect() #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>         n #>   <int64> #> 1      20

title: Developing a real pipeline locally: the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm
text: remaining code reproduces project’s code/01_run-hipps.qmd mock database. executed vignette built (downloads algorithm code lists GitHub takes little time), complete runnable — copy script run . three things differ running Workbench, marked : mock_aou_connect() replaces aou_connect(). seed_mock_pregnancies() inserts synthetic pregnancies (mock database none default). final aou_workspace_to_bucket() (Google Cloud Storage) step dropped.
code: library(tidyverse) library(allofus)     # the mock-duckdb-support branch (see installation) library(mockallofus)  # the project's algorithm code and code lists, read straight from GitHub repo <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/louisahsmith/allofus-pregnancy/main/" source_repo <- function(path) source(url(paste0(repo, path))) read_repo_xlsx <- function(path) {   tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx")   download.file(paste0(repo, path), tmp, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)   readxl::read_excel(tmp) }  # (1) connect to a local mock database instead of aou_connect() con <- mock_aou_connect()

title: Developing a real pipeline locally: the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm
text: function part adapt study. pattern simple demo , just richer: builds temporally coherent pregnancies algorithm’s date logic (gestational windows, outcome timing) something find. Read comments template manufacturing structured data different project.
code: seed_mock_pregnancies <- function(con, n_pregnancies = 250L, seed = 99L) {    # --- 0. the study's code lists -------------------------------------------   # HIP_concepts: pregnancy-related concepts, each tagged with an outcome   #   `category` (LB = live birth, DELIV = delivery, SB = stillbirth,   #   AB/SA = abortion, ECT = ectopic, PREG = gestation marker) and, for   #   gestation markers, a `gest_value` (weeks).   # PPS_concepts: "pregnancy progression signature" concepts with the   #   gestational month window in which each typically occurs.   hip <- read_repo_xlsx("data/HIP_concepts.xlsx")   pps <- read_repo_xlsx("data/PPS_concepts.xlsx") |>     mutate(domain_concept_id = as.integer(domain_concept_id))   gestation_markers <- filter(hip, category == "PREG", !is.na(gest_value))    # approximate days from conception to each outcome type, used to place the   # outcome event a realistic distance after conception   term_days <- c(LB = 270, DELIV = 270, SB = 200, AB = 70, SA = 70, ECT = 56)    # --- 1. make sure every concept EXISTS in `concept` -----------------------   # The algorithm both joins on concept_id and searches concept_name (e.g.   # str_detect(concept_name, "gestation period")), so we insert the real names.   # Concepts already present are skipped.   mock_add_concepts(con, hip$concept_id, hip$concept_name, domain_id = "Observation")   mock_add_concepts(con, pps$domain_concept_id, pps$domain_concept_name, domain_id = "Measurement")    # --- 2. choose who is "pregnant" ------------------------------------------   # Pick reproductive-age women from the existing participants (so every seeded   # event attaches to a real person_id and all downstream joins stay consistent).   set.seed(seed)   women <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "     SELECT person_id, year_of_birth, month_of_birth, day_of_birth     FROM person WHERE sex_at_birth_concept_id <> 45880669")          # <> Male   women$person_id <- as.integer(women$person_id)   preg <- women[sample(nrow(women), min(n_pregnancies, nrow(women))), ]    # --- 3. give each pregnancy a coherent timeline ---------------------------   dob <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-%02d", preg$year_of_birth,                          ifelse(is.na(preg$month_of_birth), 1, preg$month_of_birth),                          ifelse(is.na(preg$day_of_birth), 1, preg$day_of_birth)))   # conceive at age 20-38   conception <- dob + sample(20:38, nrow(preg), TRUE) * 365L + sample.int(364L, nrow(preg), TRUE)   outcome_cat <- sample(names(term_days), nrow(preg), TRUE,                         prob = c(.45, .2, .03, .12, .15, .05))   outcome_date <- conception + term_days[outcome_cat]    # --- 4. insert the events with mock_add_occurrences() ---------------------   # (a) one outcome event per pregnancy, in condition_occurrence. We pick a   #     concept_id of the chosen outcome category for each pregnancy. (sample.int   #     on the index avoids base sample()'s length-1 trap and keeps types clean.)   pick_concept <- function(cat) {     pool <- hip$concept_id[hip$category == cat]     as.integer(pool[sample.int(length(pool), 1)])   }   outcome_concept <- vapply(outcome_cat, pick_concept, integer(1))   mock_add_occurrences(con, "condition",     person_id = preg$person_id, concept_id = outcome_concept,     date = outcome_date, source = "ehr")    # (b) two gestation markers per pregnancy, in observation, placed at   #     conception + (gest_value weeks). Because mock_add_occurrences() inserts   #     one row per person_id element, we expand to two rows per person here.   gm <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(preg)), function(i) {     g <- gestation_markers[sample(nrow(gestation_markers), 2), ]     tibble(person_id = preg$person_id[i],            concept_id = g$concept_id,            date = conception[i] + as.integer(g$gest_value) * 7L,            value = as.numeric(g$gest_value))   })   mock_add_occurrences(con, "observation",     person_id = gm$person_id, concept_id = gm$concept_id,     date = gm$date, value = gm$value, source = "ehr")    # (c) one PPS marker per pregnancy, in measurement, at the midpoint of its   #     typical gestational-month window.   pm <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(preg)), function(i) {     p <- pps[sample(nrow(pps), 1), ]     tibble(person_id = preg$person_id[i],            concept_id = p$domain_concept_id,            date = conception[i] + as.integer((p$min_month + p$max_month) / 2 * 30))   })   mock_add_occurrences(con, "measurement",     person_id = pm$person_id, concept_id = pm$concept_id,     date = pm$date, source = "ehr")    invisible(nrow(preg)) }  # (2) seed synthetic pregnancies seed_mock_pregnancies(con)

title: Developing a real pipeline locally: the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm
text: code identical 01_run-hipps.qmd — tbl() references, aou_create_temp_table() / aou_compute() calls, sourced algorithm functions.
code: person_tbl               <- tbl(con, "person") concept_tbl              <- tbl(con, "concept") observation_tbl          <- tbl(con, "observation") measurement_tbl          <- tbl(con, "measurement") condition_occurrence_tbl <- tbl(con, "condition_occurrence") procedure_occurrence_tbl <- tbl(con, "procedure_occurrence") visit_occurrence_tbl     <- tbl(con, "visit_occurrence")  HIP_concepts <- read_repo_xlsx("data/HIP_concepts.xlsx") |> aou_create_temp_table() PPS_concepts <- read_repo_xlsx("data/PPS_concepts.xlsx") |>   mutate(domain_concept_id = as.integer(domain_concept_id)) |> aou_create_temp_table() Matcho_outcome_limits <- read_repo_xlsx("data/Matcho_outcome_limits.xlsx") Matcho_term_durations <- read_repo_xlsx("data/Matcho_term_durations.xlsx") |> aou_create_temp_table()  # outcome-based (HIP) episodes source_repo("code/algorithm/HIP_algorithm_functions.R") initial_pregnant_cohort_df <- initial_pregnant_cohort(   procedure_occurrence_tbl, measurement_tbl, observation_tbl,   condition_occurrence_tbl, person_tbl, HIP_concepts) |> aou_compute()  final_abortion_visits_df   <- final_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, c("AB", "SA")) |> aou_compute() final_delivery_visits_df   <- final_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, "DELIV") |> aou_compute() final_ectopic_visits_df    <- final_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, "ECT") |> aou_compute() final_stillbirth_visits_df <- final_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, "SB") |> aou_compute() final_livebirth_visits_df  <- final_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, "LB") |> aou_compute()  add_stillbirth_df <- add_stillbirth(final_stillbirth_visits_df, final_livebirth_visits_df, Matcho_outcome_limits) add_ectopic_df    <- add_ectopic(add_stillbirth_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, final_ectopic_visits_df) add_abortion_df   <- add_abortion(add_ectopic_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, final_abortion_visits_df) add_delivery_df   <- add_delivery(add_abortion_df, Matcho_outcome_limits, final_delivery_visits_df) calculate_start_df <- calculate_start(add_delivery_df, Matcho_term_durations) |> aou_compute()  gestation_visits_df     <- gestation_visits(initial_pregnant_cohort_df) gestation_episodes_df   <- gestation_episodes(gestation_visits_df) get_min_max_gestation_df <- get_min_max_gestation(gestation_episodes_df) add_gestation_df  <- add_gestation(calculate_start_df, get_min_max_gestation_df) clean_episodes_df <- clean_episodes(add_gestation_df) remove_overlaps_df <- remove_overlaps(clean_episodes_df) final_episodes_df <- final_episodes(remove_overlaps_df) HIP_episodes_df   <- final_episodes_with_length(final_episodes_df, gestation_visits_df) |> aou_compute()  # gestation-progression (PPS) episodes source_repo("code/algorithm/PPS_algorithm_functions.R") input_GT_concepts_df <- input_GT_concepts(condition_occurrence_tbl, procedure_occurrence_tbl,   observation_tbl, measurement_tbl, visit_occurrence_tbl, PPS_concepts) get_PPS_episodes_df <- get_PPS_episodes(input_GT_concepts_df, PPS_concepts, person_tbl) PPS_episodes_df     <- get_episode_max_min_dates(get_PPS_episodes_df)  # merge HIP + PPS source_repo("code/algorithm/Merge_HIPPS_episodes.R") outcomes_per_episode_df        <- outcomes_per_episode(PPS_episodes_df, get_PPS_episodes_df, initial_pregnant_cohort_df) PPS_episodes_with_outcomes_df  <- add_outcomes(outcomes_per_episode_df, PPS_episodes_df) HIP_episodes_local_df          <- collect(HIP_episodes_df) final_merged_episodes_df       <- final_merged_episodes(HIP_episodes_local_df, PPS_episodes_with_outcomes_df) final_merged_no_dup_df         <- final_merged_episodes_no_duplicates(final_merged_episodes_df) final_merged_episode_detailed_df <- final_merged_episode_detailed(final_merged_no_dup_df)  # estimated start date (ESD) metadata source_repo("code/algorithm/ESD_algorithm_functions.R") get_timing_concepts_df <- get_timing_concepts(concept_tbl, condition_occurrence_tbl, observation_tbl,   measurement_tbl, procedure_occurrence_tbl, final_merged_episode_detailed_df, PPS_concepts) episodes_with_gestational_timing_info_df <- episodes_with_gestational_timing_info(get_timing_concepts_df) merged_episodes_with_metadata_df <- merged_episodes_with_metadata(   episodes_with_gestational_timing_info_df, final_merged_episode_detailed_df, Matcho_term_durations)  nrow(merged_episodes_with_metadata_df)  # synthetic pregnancy episodes  # (3) on the Workbench you would aou_workspace_to_bucket() here; locally, just #     save to disk: # write_rds(merged_episodes_with_metadata_df, "merged_episodes_with_metadata_df.rds")  mock_aou_disconnect(con)

title: Developing a real pipeline locally: the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm
text: Running end--end touches complex Us analysis needs, works locally: tbl() + dplyr/dbplyr, aou_create_temp_table(), aou_compute(), BigQuery idioms three-argument date_diff(, b, sql("day")). Workbench-specific piece final bucket upload, local equivalent.

title: Getting started with mockallofus
text: change Workbench script connection call. mock_aou_connect() builds local DuckDB database (first use) stores getOption("aou.default.con") — exactly allofus looks — every allofus function works unchanged. database already synthetic participants broad spread generic clinical events survey responses, ordinary dplyr/dbplyr works right away: study’s specific concepts. query particular concept_id survey question simply come back empty — seeding comes .
code: library(mockallofus) library(allofus) suppressMessages(library(dplyr))  con <- mock_aou_connect() tbl(con, "person") |> tally() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 1] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure:R 4.6.0//home/runner/.cache/R/mockallofus/mock_aou.duckdb] #>         n #>   <int64> #> 1    2000  tbl(con, "measurement") |>   summarise(n = n(), n_people = n_distinct(person_id)) #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure:R 4.6.0//home/runner/.cache/R/mockallofus/mock_aou.duckdb] #>         n n_people #>   <int64>  <int64> #> 1   10002     1819

title: Getting started with mockallofus
text: ’ll reproduce eligibility logic allofus paper’s case study, builds cohort electronic health record (EHR) concept sets survey responses. First, seed concepts analysis looks . mock_seed_concept_set() workhorse: give concept ids domain pass allofus::aou_concept_set(), plus prevalence (fraction participants concept). Keeping prevalence 1 makes eligibility joins return partial cohort — people qualify, don’t — want developing filtering logic. Survey questions seeded mock_seed_survey(), using question concept ids pass allofus::aou_survey(). ordinary survey questions family-health-history questions work: seed health-history question (type 2 diabetes, concept id 43529932) plus education income. health-history questions, hh_yes sets fraction answer “Yes”.
code: # devices, a condition/drug set, and a lab test (with values in a chosen range) mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(2616672, 3034639), domain = "device",                       prevalence = 0.15, seed = 1) mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(201826, 4193704), domain = "condition",                       prevalence = 0.20, seed = 2) mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(40164929, 40164897), domain = "drug",                       prevalence = 0.20, seed = 3) mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(3004410, 3005673), domain = "measurement",                       prevalence = 0.30, values = c(4, 11), seed = 4) mock_seed_survey(con, c(43529932, 1585940, 1585375),                  prevalence = 0.8, hh_yes = 0.3, seed = 5)

title: Getting started with mockallofus
text: code ordinary allofus. Build eligibility cohort: everyone whose full year first survey falls within observation period. Pull EHR concept sets year survey exclude participants device repeated high lab values: Finally, attach survey data keep participants report type 2 diabetes: whole workflow: connect, seed concepts/questions study uses, run real allofus code.
code: observation_period_tbl <- aou_observation_period()  survey_date_tbl <- tbl(con, "ds_survey") |>   summarize(first_survey = as.Date(min(survey_datetime)), .by = "person_id") |>   mutate(year_before_survey = DATE_ADD(first_survey, sql(paste("INTERVAL", -1, "year"))))  eligible <- survey_date_tbl |>   aou_join(observation_period_tbl, type = "inner",            by = join_by(person_id,                         within(year_before_survey, first_survey,                                observation_period_start_date,                                observation_period_end_date)))  tally(eligible) #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 1] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure:R 4.6.0//home/runner/.cache/R/mockallofus/mock_aou.duckdb] #>         n #>   <int64> #> 1    1805 device <- aou_concept_set(eligible, c(2616672, 3034639),                           start_date = "year_before_survey", end_date = "first_survey",                           output = "indicator", concept_set_name = "device",                           domains = "device") |>   filter(device == 1) |>   distinct(person_id)  lab_test <- aou_concept_set(eligible, c(3004410, 3005673),                             start_date = "year_before_survey", end_date = "first_survey",                             output = "all", concept_set_name = "lab_test",                             domains = "measurement") |>   filter(value_as_number > 6.5) |>   summarise(n = n(), .by = "person_id") |>   filter(n >= 2) |>   distinct(person_id)  ineligible <- list(device, lab_test) |>   purrr::reduce(union_all) |>   aou_compute()  eligible <- anti_join(eligible, ineligible, by = "person_id") survey_data <- aou_survey(eligible, questions = c(43529932, 1585940, 1585375),                           question_output = c("t2dm", "edu", "income"))  eligible <- survey_data |>   filter(t2dm == "No") |>   aou_join(eligible, type = "inner", by = "person_id")  cohort <- collect(eligible) nrow(cohort) #> [1] 975 head(cohort) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   person_id t2dm  income    edu   t2dm_date  income_date edu_date   first_survey #>     <int64> <chr> <chr>     <chr> <date>     <date>      <date>     <date>       #> 1   1000002 No    AnnualIn… High… 2022-09-30 2018-03-31  2022-02-28 2018-12-20   #> 2   1000003 No    AnnualIn… High… 2021-03-19 2018-12-12  2019-08-15 2019-10-07   #> 3   1000004 No    AnnualIn… High… 2017-08-20 2019-08-03  2019-05-02 2019-08-09   #> 4   1000005 No    NA        High… 2020-01-26 NA          2020-04-15 2020-10-06   #> 5   1000007 No    NA        PMI_… 2020-02-21 NA          2020-03-22 2019-10-15   #> 6   1000010 No    NA        High… 2017-11-21 NA          2022-05-31 2020-03-04   #> # ℹ 3 more variables: year_before_survey <date>, #> #   observation_period_start_date <date>, observation_period_end_date <date>

title: Getting started with mockallofus
text: mock_seed_concept_set() scatters concept randomly across participants. need exact control — specific people, specific dates, temporally structured events — use lower-level primitives: mock_add_concepts(con, concept_id, concept_name, domain_id) — add concept definitions (real names, name-based lookups work). mock_add_occurrences(con, domain, person_id, concept_id, date, value, source) — add events domain table, generating keys matching _ext source rows automatically. mock_person_ids(con) — participant ids attach data . worked example builds temporally coherent cohort primitives — HIPPS pregnancy algorithm run end--end mock database — see vignette("hipps-pregnancy-example").
code: ids <- mock_person_ids(con) mock_add_concepts(con, 99999001, "My study's condition", domain_id = "Condition") mock_add_occurrences(con, "condition", person_id = ids[1:50],                      concept_id = 99999001, date = as.Date("2021-03-15"))

title: Getting started with mockallofus
text: allofus workflows run unchanged; common BigQuery idioms translated automatically; Workbench/cloud features (bucket /O, aou_atlas_cohort()) local equivalent. full breakdown, see ’s different Researcher Workbench.

what: Build a database once, reuse it
title: Reproducible and portable workflows
text: mock_aou_connect() builds default database first use caches . reproducible project usually want explicit, version-pinned database instead: control size random seed, build known path, seed study’s data, reuse . Generation deterministic, seed always yields database. database file, seeded data persists. can disconnect reconnect later (another session, another script) data still — build seed , iterate analysis code stable dataset. build_mock_db() nothing file already exists; pass overwrite = TRUE rebuild. default cache location default_mock_db_path(). common project setup small script builds seeds database (committed repo), .duckdb file git-ignored rebuilt demand.
code: db_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".duckdb") # for a real project, a stable path  build_mock_db(db_path, n_persons = 500, seed = 42, quiet = TRUE)  con <- mock_aou_connect(db_path, quiet = TRUE) mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(201826, 4193704), domain = "condition",                       prevalence = 0.3, seed = 1, quiet = TRUE) mock_aou_disconnect(con)  con <- mock_aou_connect(db_path, quiet = TRUE) # reopen the same file tbl(con, "condition_occurrence") |>   filter(condition_concept_id %in% c(201826, 4193704)) |>   summarise(people = n_distinct(person_id)) #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 1] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure:R 4.6.0//tmp/RtmpxFqVmg/file1ec410a9d9eb.duckdb] #>    people #>   <int64> #> 1     345

title: Reproducible and portable workflows
text: aou_sql() works locally long SQL portable. {CDR} template resolves local schema, just resolves curated data repository Workbench. BigQuery-SQL functions (COUNTIF, APPROX_QUANTILES, …) run local backend; couple common ones (CONTAINS_SUBSTR, REGEXP_CONTAINS) emulated. Keep raw SQL portable, guard Workbench-specific queries (see next section). dplyr/dbplyr code, translated per-backend, portable automatically.
code: aou_sql("   SELECT gender_concept_id, COUNT(*) AS n   FROM `{CDR}.person`   GROUP BY gender_concept_id ", collect = TRUE) #>   gender_concept_id   n #> 1          45878463 241 #> 2          45880669 259

title: Reproducible and portable workflows
text: allofus::on_workbench() reports whether Researcher Workbench. Route connection script connects real data Workbench mock database locally: Write analysis function connection (defaulting stored option, exactly allofus functions ). Nothing inside mock-specific: Locally, seed inputs run ; Workbench, identical call runs real data.
code: connect_aou <- function() {   if (on_workbench()) {     aou_connect()          # real All of Us data (BigQuery)   } else {     mock_aou_connect()     # local synthetic data (DuckDB)   } } high_a1c_cohort <- function(threshold = 6.5, con = getOption("aou.default.con")) {   aou_concept_set(concepts = c(3004410, 3005673), domains = "measurement",                   output = "all", concept_set_name = "a1c", con = con) |>     filter(value_as_number > threshold) |>     distinct(person_id) } mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(3004410, 3005673), domain = "measurement",                       prevalence = 0.4, values = c(5, 11), seed = 3, quiet = TRUE)  high_a1c_cohort(con = con) |> tally() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 1] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [unknown@Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure:R 4.6.0//tmp/RtmpxFqVmg/file1ec410a9d9eb.duckdb] #>         n #>   <int64> #> 1     359

what: Test analysis code without the Workbench
title: Reproducible and portable workflows
text: mock database deterministic local, can unit-test analysis functions CI. typical testthat setup builds seeds small database, checks function’s behavior data control. example, seeding A1c values c(5, 11) known prevalence lets assert high-A1c cohort non-empty everyone: gives fast, offline, reproducible test suite code ultimately runs real Us data — catching regressions spend Workbench time.
code: library(testthat)  test_that("high_a1c_cohort selects participants above the threshold", {   # in a package, this would live in tests/testthat/ with the db built in setup   path <- withr::local_tempfile(fileext = ".duckdb")   build_mock_db(path, n_persons = 300, seed = 99, quiet = TRUE)   con <- mock_aou_connect(path, quiet = TRUE)   withr::defer(mock_aou_disconnect(con))   mock_seed_concept_set(con, c(3004410, 3005673), domain = "measurement",                         prevalence = 0.5, values = c(5, 11), seed = 1, quiet = TRUE)    result <- high_a1c_cohort(con = con) |> collect()    expect_s3_class(result, "data.frame")   expect_true("person_id" %in% names(result))   expect_gt(nrow(result), 0)   expect_lt(nrow(result), 300) }) #> Test passed.

title: Reproducible and portable workflows
text: Build deterministic database build_mock_db(path, n_persons, seed) reuse file across sessions. Keep raw aou_sql() portable; dplyr/dbplyr portable automatically. Route connections on_workbench() one script runs places. Test connection-taking analysis functions seeded mock database CI.

title: What's different from the Researcher Workbench
text: important difference data . mock database contains randomly generated, synthetic data resemble real Us data (using real data violate data use agreement), : Results meaningful — goal exercise code, learn anything pregnancy, diabetes, anything else. contain study’s concepts default. query specific concept_id survey question returns nothing seed . feature: control exactly concepts exist prevalence. concept table small mock vocabulary, full OMOP vocabulary, concept_ancestor / concept_relationship empty default. need real vocabulary — example, develop descendant/ancestor queries locally instead Workbench — download vocabulary files Athena load [mock_load_vocabulary()].

title: What's different from the Researcher Workbench
text: run locally exactly Workbench (con comes mock_aou_connect() instead aou_connect()): Us OMOP/CDM tables exist mock database (table references resolve), core clinical, survey, cohort-builder tables populated.

title: What's different from the Researcher Workbench
text: mock_aou_connect() attaches translations macros common BigQuery idioms Us code run DuckDB without changes: Case matter. Like SQL, function names case-insensitive: write DATE_DIFF() date_diff(), DATE_ADD() date_add(), . dbplyr passes name straight SQL, case irrelevant, whichever case appears code (examples ) works.

title: What's different from the Researcher Workbench
text: rely Workbench/cloud infrastructure local equivalent. scope design — guard allofus::on_workbench() (see “reproducible workflows” article). local file output, use readr::write_*() / saveRDS() place bucket helpers.

title: What's different from the Researcher Workbench
text: Connection class. mock_aou_connect() returns DuckDB connection (S4 subclass, mock_aou_connection), BigQueryConnection. Code branches connection class use allofus::on_workbench() instead. Single writer. DuckDB file allows one read-write connection time; disconnect (mock_aou_disconnect()) reopening elsewhere. Determinism. Database generation seeding deterministic fixed seed, tests examples reproducible.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Louisa Smith. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {mockallofus: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses},   author = {Louisa Smith},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus}, }

what: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: local mock database developing Us analyses without paid Researcher Workbench. allofus R package wraps access Us Research Program data, lives Google BigQuery reachable (metered) Researcher Workbench. makes iterative code development — especially students rerun constantly — slow expensive. mockallofus builds local DuckDB database whose tables columns mirror Us Curated Data Repository (OMOP CDM core tables plus Us extension cohort-builder tables), populated fully synthetic, randomly generated data. can develop test allofus analysis code locally run Workbench. synthetic data random reflect real Us data (violate data use agreement). Results meant realistic — point make code run.

title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: mockallofus currently requires development version allofus mock-duckdb-support branch, adds backend-aware query execution lets allofus functions run DuckDB rather BigQuery. (work merged released, released allofus work.) mockallofus declares requirement Remotes: field, installing (dependencies, e.g. CI) pulls correct allofus branch automatically.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("roux-ohdsi/allofus@mock-duckdb-support")  # required allofus branch pak::pak("louisahsmith/mockallofus")                # installs duckdb too

title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: thing changes versus Workbench script connection call: use mock_aou_connect() instead allofus::aou_connect(). Everything downstream identical.
code: library(allofus) library(mockallofus) library(dplyr)  con <- mock_aou_connect()   # builds the database on first run, then caches it  # ...the same code you'd run on the Workbench: tbl(con, "person") |> count() |> collect()  cohort <- tbl(con, "person") |> select(person_id)  aou_concept_set(cohort, concepts = c(201826, 4193704), domains = "condition",                 output = "indicator", concept_set_name = "t2dm")  aou_survey(questions = c(1585838, 1586135),            question_output = c("gender", "birthplace"))  aou_observation_period()

what: Making your study’s queries return data
title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: small default database means queries specific concepts eligibility criteria can come back empty, harder develop data . Rather making database enormous, seed concepts study uses, prevalence choose, eligibility joins return partial cohorts: Seeding deterministic (fixed seed) attaches existing participants, downstream joins stay consistent.
code: # guarantee these condition concepts exist for ~30% of participants mock_seed_concept_set(con, concepts = c(201826, 4193704),                       domain = "condition", prevalence = 0.3)  # guarantee responses to specific survey questions (real codebook concept ids) mock_seed_survey(con, concept_ids = c(1585838, 1586135), prevalence = 0.6)

what: What works locally, and what doesn’t
title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: allofus functions pure dbplyr (translates DuckDB automatically) package’s internal query helper backend-aware, large majority workflows run unchanged. Works locally dplyr/dbplyr tbl(con, ...), aou_join(), aou_collect() aou_concept_set() aou_survey() (real codebook question concept ids) aou_observation_period() aou_compute(), aou_create_temp_table() aou_sql() — provided SQL portable. BigQuery-constructs (COUNTIF, APPROX_QUANTILES, etc.) run; common ones (CONTAINS_SUBSTR, REGEXP_CONTAINS) emulated via DuckDB macros. Workbench-(clearly-labeled exceptions) aou_atlas_cohort() — relies BigQuery-dialect SQL generated SqlRender. File/bucket management (aou_ls_workspace(), aou_ls_bucket(), aou_workspace_to_bucket(), aou_bucket_to_workspace()) — use Google Cloud Storage, local equivalent. aou_session_info() CDR-metadata details. User-written SQL uses BigQuery-specific functions.

title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: build_mock_db() creates every table Us data dictionary (allofus::aou_table_info) populates core subset synthetic rows. EHR-sourced rows tagged _ext tables EHR-based functions (like aou_observation_period()) behave correctly. mock_aou_connect() opens DuckDB database, registers BigQuery-compatibility macros, attaches BigQuery-compatible dbplyr translations, stores connection getOption("aou.default.con") schema name getOption("aou.default.cdr") — exactly options allofus reads. BigQuery-isms common Us code handled pipelines run unchanged: three-argument date_diff(, b, sql("day")), .Date() float-valued concatenated date parts (e.g. "1994-5.0-28.0"), collect(page_size = ...) download argument. complex real-world pipelines (e.g. HIPPS pregnancy algorithm) run end--end mock database seeding concept lists.

title: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses
text: project affiliated endorsed Us Research Program. data generates synthetic represent real participants.

what: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
title: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
text: Creates DuckDB database file whose tables columns mirror Us Curated Data Repository (OMOP CDM core tables plus Us extension cohort-builder tables), populated fully synthetic, randomly generated data. data reflect real Us data results meant realistic; purpose let allofus analysis code run developed locally.

title: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
code: build_mock_db(   path = default_mock_db_path(),   n_persons = 2000L,   seed = 20240101L,   survey_questions = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
text: path File path DuckDB database. Defaults cached location tools::R_user_dir(). n_persons Number synthetic participants generate. Default 2000. seed Random seed reproducible generation. Default 20240101. survey_questions Optional integer vector survey question concept ids (allofus::aou_codebook) populate observation. Defaults sample includes vignette questions (gender 1585838, birthplace 1586135). overwrite Overwrite existing database file. Default FALSE (existing file path left untouched path returned). quiet Suppress progress messages.

title: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
text: path built database, invisibly.

title: Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database — build_mock_db
text: tables Us data dictionary created (table references resolve); core subset populated data: person, concept, clinical domain tables _ext tables, survey rows observation, observation_period, cb_search_person, ds_survey, survey_conduct. clinical concept ids used allofus vignettes included documented workflows return data. Use mock_seed_concept_set() / mock_seed_survey() afterwards guarantee coverage specific concepts study queries.

what: Collect method that tolerates BigQuery-only download arguments — db_collect.mock_aou_connection
title: Collect method that tolerates BigQuery-only download arguments — db_collect.mock_aou_connection
text: Us code often calls collect(page_size = ...), page_size bigrquery download argument. DuckDB's collect() rejects unknown ... arguments, drop BigQuery-specific ones collecting.

code: # S3 method for class 'mock_aou_connection' db_collect(con, sql, n = -1, warn_incomplete = TRUE, ...)

text: con mock_aou_connection. sql, n, warn_incomplete, ... Passed underlying DBI collect method.

what: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
title: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
text: Inserts rows concept table concept_ids already present. Use concept-name lookups (e.g. searching concept_name) joins concept_id find study's concepts. Concepts already present skipped, safe call repeatedly.

title: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
code: mock_add_concepts(   con,   concept_id,   concept_name = NULL,   domain_id = "Observation" )

title: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
text: con connection mock_aou_connect(). concept_id Integer vector concept ids. concept_name Optional character vector names (parallel concept_id). NULL, generic placeholder names used. Supply real names code searches concept_name (e.g. str_detect()). domain_id Value domain_id column (e.g. "Condition", "Drug").

title: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
text: number concept rows inserted, invisibly.

title: Add concept definitions to the mock database — mock_add_concepts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- mock_aou_connect() mock_add_concepts(con, c(201826, 4193704),                   c("Type 2 diabetes mellitus", "T2DM without complication"),                   domain_id = "Condition") } # }

what: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
text: Inserts rows one OMOP clinical-event domain tables, generating surrogate keys automatically writing matching _ext row (Us uses record data source: EHR vs survey/physical-measurement). primitive putting custom events mock database — e.g. giving specific set participants specific concept specific dates.

title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
code: mock_add_occurrences(   con,   domain,   person_id,   concept_id,   date,   value = NULL,   source = c("ehr", "ppi") )

title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
text: con connection mock_aou_connect(). domain One "condition", "drug", "measurement", "observation", "procedure", "device", "visit". person_id Integer vector participant ids (one per occurrence). concept_id Integer concept id(s); recycled length(person_id). date Event date(s) (Date coercible); recycled length(person_id). Used domain's start date (end date, table one). value Optional numeric value(s) written value_as_number table column (e.g. measurements, observations). source "ehr" tags _ext row EHR-sourced (src_id like "EHR site 105", EHR-based functions allofus::aou_observation_period() look ); "ppi" tags survey/physical-measurement ("PPI/PM").

title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
text: generated surrogate ids, invisibly.

title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
text: person_id, concept_id, date treated row-wise (recycled common length), one call inserts one occurrence per person_id element.

title: Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database — mock_add_occurrences
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- mock_aou_connect() ids <- mock_person_ids(con)[1:50] mock_add_concepts(con, 201826, "Type 2 diabetes mellitus", domain_id = "Condition") mock_add_occurrences(con, "condition", person_id = ids, concept_id = 201826,                      date = as.Date("2021-03-15")) } # }

what: Connect to the local mock All of Us database — mock_aou_connect
title: Connect to the local mock All of Us database — mock_aou_connect
text: Opens (building first necessary) local mock DuckDB database wires allofus package way allofus::aou_connect() Researcher Workbench: connection stored getOption("aou.default.con") curated-data-repository name getOption("aou.default.cdr"). calling , allofus analysis code relies defaults runs mock database unchanged.

title: Connect to the local mock All of Us database — mock_aou_connect
code: mock_aou_connect(   path = default_mock_db_path(),   cdr = "main",   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Connect to the local mock All of Us database — mock_aou_connect
text: path Path DuckDB database file. Defaults cached location (default_mock_db_path()); built build_mock_db() absent. cdr schema name used {CDR} interpolation allofus SQL helpers. Defaults "main" (DuckDB's default schema, mock tables live), references like `{CDR}.person` resolve. quiet Suppress connection message. ... arguments passed DBI::dbConnect().

title: Connect to the local mock All of Us database — mock_aou_connect
text: DuckDB connection object (duckdb_connection), also stored getOption("aou.default.con").

text: BigQuery-compatibility macros registered connection (see register_bq_macros()) SQL emitted allofus BigQuery dialect executes DuckDB.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- mock_aou_connect() # the same code you would run on the Workbench: dplyr::tbl(con, "person") } # }

what: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
text: default mock database ships tiny placeholder concept table empty vocabulary tables — enough run analyses, enough explore concept relationships. workflow needs real vocabulary (example, finding descendants ancestors via concept_ancestor), download vocabulary files OHDSI Athena repository load function. can develop test ancestor/descendant queries locally instead (metered) Workbench.

title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
code: mock_load_vocabulary(con, dir, tables = NULL, delim = "\t", quiet = FALSE)

title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
text: con connection mock_aou_connect(). dir Directory containing Athena vocabulary CSV files. tables vocabulary tables load. Defaults found among concept, concept_ancestor, concept_relationship, concept_synonym, vocabulary, domain, concept_class, relationship, drug_strength. delim Field delimiter files (Athena uses tab, default). quiet Suppress summary message.

title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
text: names tables loaded, invisibly.

title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
text: requested table replaced contents matching file, call seeding custom (non-real) concepts. files tab-delimited CSVs Athena provides (CONCEPT.csv, CONCEPT_ANCESTOR.csv, ...); matching case-insensitive. Athena vocabulary files large; loading done DuckDB directly disk, fast, resulting database file grow accordingly.

title: Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database — mock_load_vocabulary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- mock_aou_connect() # after downloading + unzipping an Athena vocabulary bundle: mock_load_vocabulary(con, "~/Downloads/athena-vocab")  # now descendant/ancestor queries work locally library(dplyr) tbl(con, "concept_ancestor") |>   filter(ancestor_concept_id == 201826L) |>   inner_join(tbl(con, "concept"),              by = c("descendant_concept_id" = "concept_id")) } # }

what: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
title: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
text: Guarantees given concepts exist (concept occurrences appropriate domain table, matching EHR _ext rows) fraction participants. Use allofus::aou_concept_set() queries study's concepts return non-empty, develop-able results. Call concepts/domain pass aou_concept_set().

title: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
code: mock_seed_concept_set(   con,   concepts,   domain = "condition",   prevalence = 0.3,   max_per_person = 3L,   values = c(0, 100),   seed = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
text: con connection mock_aou_connect(). concepts Integer vector concept ids seed. domain One "condition", "drug", "measurement", "observation", "procedure", "device", "visit". prevalence Fraction participants (0-1) least one occurrence. Values 1 make eligibility joins return partial cohorts. max_per_person Maximum occurrences generated per selected participant. values domain = "measurement", length-2 numeric range value_as_number. Ignored domains. seed Optional random seed reproducible seeding. quiet Suppress summary message.

title: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
text: number occurrence rows inserted, invisibly.

title: Seed a concept set into the mock database — mock_seed_concept_set
text: finer control (specific participants, specific dates), use lower-level mock_add_occurrences() / mock_add_concepts() directly.

what: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
title: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
text: Inserts observation rows (PPI _ext rows) given survey question concept ids, allofus::aou_survey() returns data . Call question concept ids pass aou_survey().

title: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
code: mock_seed_survey(   con,   concept_ids,   answers = NULL,   prevalence = 0.8,   hh_yes = 0.5,   seed = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
text: con connection mock_aou_connect(). concept_ids Integer vector survey question concept ids (regular /health-history "specific" concept ids). answers Optional character vector answer codes (value_source_value) sample , regular questions. NULL, uses codebook choices question. prevalence Fraction participants (0-1) respond question. hh_yes health-history questions, fraction responders answer "Yes" (condition). Ignored regular questions. seed Optional random seed. quiet Suppress summary message.

title: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
text: number observation rows inserted, invisibly.

title: Seed survey responses into the mock database — mock_seed_survey
text: Two kinds survey questions handled automatically: Regular questions (allofus::aou_codebook): response inserted value_source_value drawn question's answer choices (answers). Family-health-history questions ("specific" condition/person concept ids allofus::aou_health_history, e.g. 43529932 "type 2 diabetes / self"): responses inserted nested structure aou_survey() expects, returns "Yes"/"". fraction answering "Yes" controlled hh_yes.

what: mockallofus: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses — mockallofus-package
title: mockallofus: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses — mockallofus-package
text: Builds local DuckDB database fully synthetic data mirrors table column structure Us Research Program Curated Data Repository (OMOP CDM plus Us extensions). Lets users develop test 'allofus' package analysis code locally, without paid Researcher Workbench. synthetic data randomly generated reflect real Us data. analysis code intended run mock database locally real data Workbench, small set clearly-documented exceptions.

title: mockallofus: A Local Mock Database for Developing All of Us Analyses — mockallofus-package
text: Maintainer: Louisa Smith l.smith@northeastern.edu
